messages.content:
U bought 1 male and 1 female goat from S. S knew that U bought these goats so that he can start his own poultry shop. Later on, the male goat died because it was already suffering from a disease since it was born. What is the warrant breached?
(1 Point)


A. Warranty as to Fitness or quality of goods

B. Warranty against hidden defects

C. Warranty against redhibitory defects

D. Warranties provided in the Consumer act

This scenario describes a breach of the warranty against hidden defects. The male goat had a pre-existing disease that was not apparent at the time of purchase, making it a hidden defect. The breach occurred because the goat did not meet the expected quality or fitness for its intended purpose. Therefore, the correct answer is B. Warranty against hidden defects.

P was renting a condominium unit in Quezon City. P’s landlord is X. In their contract of lease, there is a right of first refusal given to P. When P has completed his education, he chose to go back to his province in Bulacan. To make sure that the condominium unit is used properly, X planned to redesign the unit and live in it. P was informed about this so he told X that he is now going to exercise his right of first refusal. Will this prosper?
(1 Point)


A. Yes. P being the lessee may choose to exercise his right of first refusal anytime he wants.

B. Yes. P may exercise the right of first refusal as long as he paid all his rents without any delay.

C. No. Right of first refusal is only applicable if the intention is to sell to third parties.

D. No. X may choose not to allow P to exercise the right of first refusal because he remains to be the owner of the property.

The correct answer is D. No. X may choose not to allow P to exercise the right of first refusal because he remains the owner of the property. The right of first refusal typically applies when the landlord intends to sell the property to a third party. In this scenario, X is not planning to sell the property, but rather intends to redesign and live in it themselves. Therefore, P's right of first refusal does not apply in this situation.

What is the law that governs personal, family, household or agricultural things and indicated that the presentment to the immediate seller of the warranty card or the official receipt along with the product to be serviced or return is sufficient to enforce the warranty provided in said law?
(1 Point)



A. Consumption Act

B. Consumer Act

C. Constitution Act

D. Consumable Act

The correct answer is B. Consumer Act. The Consumer Act is the law that governs personal, family, household, or agricultural things in the Philippines. It provides various protections and provisions for consumers, including warranties for products. According to the law, presenting the warranty card or official receipt to the immediate seller is sufficient to enforce the warranty provided. Therefore, the correct answer is B. Consumer Act.

A sold to F a lotto ticket which A said “Buy this lotto ticket because it contains the six winning combinations.” F bought the lotto ticket. What is this kind of transaction?
(1 Point)



A. Emptio Rei Speratae

B. Emptio Spei

C. Reciprocal Sale

D. None of the above

The correct answer is B. Emptio Spei. 

In this scenario, A sold a lotto ticket to F by stating that it contains the six winning combinations. Emptio Spei refers to a contract of sale where the seller guarantees the occurrence of an uncertain event, such as winning the lottery. By selling the lotto ticket to F with the claim that it contains the winning combinations, A is implying a guarantee of the uncertain event. 

Therefore, the correct answer is B. Emptio Spei.

A, B, C, and D were the children of X. They are about to inherit a 500-hectare lot. One month after the death of X, A was able to sell his portion of the lot to F. Later on, the entire lot was sold to S. A did not sign in the contract of sale but it was his wife who signed for him. Is there a double sale?
(1 Point)


A. Yes, the subject matter of the two sales were the same 500-hectare lot.

B. Yes, the two sale transactions were both valid sales.

C. No, the buyers do not have any conflicting interests in the property.

D. No, the sale was made by different sellers.

The correct answer is C. No, the buyers do not have any conflicting interests in the property.

In this scenario, A was able to sell his portion of the lot to F one month after the death of X. Later on, the entire lot was sold to S, but A did not sign in the contract of sale and it was his wife who signed for him. However, there is no double sale in this case because the buyers, F and S, do not have any conflicting interests in the property. A sold his portion to F, and then the remaining portion was sold to S. The two sale transactions involved different portions of the lot and different buyers, so there is no conflict or double sale. Therefore, the correct answer is C. No, the buyers do not have any conflicting interests in the property.

Kristy is selling tomatoes in the market. One customer offered to pay her tilapia in exchange of 1 kilo of tomatoes. Kristy took pity for the customer and agreed. What element of sale is not present?
(1 Point)


 a. Consent

b. determinate or determinable subject matter

c. Price certain in money

d. none of the above

The correct answer is c. Price certain in money.

In this scenario, Kristy agreed to exchange 1 kilo of tomatoes for a tilapia. However, the element of a "price certain in money" is not present. Instead of receiving a specific monetary amount as payment, Kristy agreed to receive a tilapia. 

Therefore, the correct answer is c. Price certain in money.

Statement 1: If the seller is not the owner at the time of the delivery of the subject matter, the sale is void.
  
Statement 2: The statute of fraud requires all contracts of sale to be in a written form to prevent fraud and perjury.
(1 Point)


A. Only statement 1 is correct

B. Only statement 2 is correct

C. Both statements are correct

D. Both statements are not correct

The correct answer is D. Both statements are not correct.

Statement 1 is not entirely accurate. If the seller is not the owner at the time of the delivery, it does not automatically render the sale void. There are circumstances where a seller can validly sell goods that they do not own, such as when they have the authority to sell on behalf of the owner or when there is a valid contract in place.

Statement 2 is also not entirely accurate. The Statute of Frauds does not require all contracts of sale to be in written form. The Statute of Frauds is a legal doctrine that requires certain types of contracts to be in writing to be enforceable, such as contracts involving the sale of real estate or contracts that cannot be performed within one year.

Therefore, the correct answer is D. Both statements are not correct.

B entered into a contract of sale or return with X. While B was testing out the subject matter, it was struck by lightning which caused the subject matter to burn. Who will bear the damages of the subject matter?
(1 Point)


A. B

B. X

C. Proportionately shared by B and X

D. None of the above

The correct answer is D. None of the above.

In a sale or return agreement, the buyer (B) has the right to return the subject matter if they are not satisfied with it. In this scenario, while B was testing out the subject matter, it was struck by lightning and burned. 

Generally, in such situations, the risk of loss or damage to the subject matter rests with the seller (X) until the buyer (B) accepts the goods. Since B was in the process of testing the goods and did not formally accept them, the risk of loss or damage would still be with the seller, X.

Therefore, the correct answer is D. None of the above. X (the seller) would bear the damages of the subject matter.

What is the warranty that requires that the goods shall be of a merchantable quality, when they are bought by description from a seller who deals in goods of such description?
(1 Point)


A. Warranty as to Fitness or quality of goods

B. Warranty against hidden defects

C. Warranty against redhibitory defects

D. Warranties provided in the Consumer act

The correct answer is A. Warranty as to Fitness or quality of goods.

This warranty requires that the goods must be of a merchantable quality when purchased by description from a seller who deals in goods of such description. It means that the goods should be fit for their intended purpose, in acceptable condition, and conform to the description given by the seller.

Therefore, the correct answer is A. Warranty as to Fitness or quality of goods.

The obligation of the seller is to transfer ownership to the buyer. In turn, the buyer has the obligation to pay the purchase price. What characteristic of sale is applicable in this case?
(1 Point)


A. Bilateral

B. Principal

C. Consensual

D. Reciprocal

The correct answer is D. Reciprocal.

In this case, the obligation of the seller to transfer ownership to the buyer and the obligation of the buyer to pay the purchase price create a reciprocal characteristic of the sale. Reciprocal obligations are those that arise from the same cause and are dependent on each other. The transfer of ownership is dependent on the buyer's payment of the purchase price, and the buyer's payment is dependent on the seller's transfer of ownership.

Therefore, the correct answer is D. Reciprocal.

Trina bought a jewelry worth P3,000,000.00 from Heart. Both agreed with everything about the ssale. Then one day, Trina demanded for a contract of sale to formalize their transaction. Heart said there is no need for a contract of sale as long as they agreed with everything. Trina threatened to reject the offer. Which of the statements is correct?
(1 Point)



A. Written contract is an element of a contract of sale when the subject is worth P5,000 and above

B. Written contract is not an element of sale

C. Written contract when demanded becomes an element of sale

D. Written contract must be made by the buyer not the seller.

The correct answer is B. Written contract is not an element of sale.

In general, a written contract is not an essential element of a contract of sale. Sales can be conducted orally or even through implied agreements. While a written contract can provide clarity and serve as evidence of the agreement, it is not required for a valid contract of sale. As long as there is an offer, acceptance, consideration (price), and agreement on the essential terms of the sale (such as the subject matter and price), a valid contract of sale can be formed.

Therefore, the correct answer is B. Written contract is not an element of sale.

What is referred to as the characteristic of a contract of sale which means that it can stand on its own?
(1 Point)


A. Nominate

B. Principal

C. Consensual

D. Bilateral

The correct answer is A. Nominate.

Nominate refers to the characteristic of a contract of sale, indicating that it is a distinct and identifiable type of contract that can stand on its own. A contract of sale is a specific type of contract that involves the transfer of ownership of goods from a seller to a buyer in exchange for a price.

Therefore, the correct answer is A. Nominate.

The seller does not want to deliver the subject matter because the buyer has not yet paid. In the contract of sale, there was no mention that the buyer must first pay the purchase price. Is the contract of sale valid?
(1 Point)


A. Yes. The sale has already been perfected.

B. Yes, provided that the sale must be in a written contract so that it can be enforced by the parties.

C. No. Both parties have incurred delay in the performance of their obligations.

D. No. The seller has incurred a breach to the obligation so the buyer can now cancel the obligation.

The correct answer is D. No. The seller has incurred a breach to the obligation so the buyer can now cancel the obligation.

In a contract of sale, unless otherwise agreed upon, the general rule is that the buyer is not required to pay the purchase price before the seller delivers the subject matter. The seller cannot refuse to deliver the subject matter on the grounds of non-payment if it was not explicitly stated in the contract.

By refusing to deliver the subject matter, the seller is breaching their obligation under the contract. A breach of obligation by one party can provide grounds for the other party to cancel the contract.

Therefore, the correct answer is D. No. The seller has incurred a breach to the obligation so the buyer can now cancel the obligation.
